title: Write trigger script to dispatch a plan phase to the developer agent
description: Create scripts/dispatch.sh, called from the orchestrator pane (tab 1) to signal the developer agent (tab 2) to start executing a specific plan-phase beads issue. Usage: ./scripts/dispatch.sh <issue-id>. The script should: (1) validate the issue exists and is of type plan-phase, (2) mark the issue in_progress in beads, (3) use tmux send-keys to inject the start command into the developer pane so the agent picks it up and begins execution.
close_reason: Created scripts/dispatch.sh — validates issue, marks in_progress, injects start command into developer pane

title: Design beads workflow for multi-agent coordination
description: Define the issue types, status flow, and memory conventions agents will use to communicate. Types needed: plan-phase (user writes), test-report (tester writes), debug-session (debugger writes), validation (user confirms). Status flow: open → in_progress → blocked/needs-refinement → closed. Document the conventions in a beads-workflow.org file.
close_reason: Created beads-workflow.org — issue types, status flow, memory conventions, dispatch flow diagram

title: Rename workspace panes to match agent roles
description: Update config/workspace.conf and workspace.sh to rename the 4 panes from (orchestrator, agent-1, agent-2, monitor) to (orchestrator, developer, tester, debugger) to reflect their actual roles in the multi-agent workflow.
close_reason: Renamed panes to orchestrator/developer/tester/debugger in workspace.conf and workspace.sh

title: Create tmux-based multi-agent workspace skeleton
description: Build the initial project skeleton for a reusable multi-agent coding workspace. The centralized interface is tmux. A single call to the main script should open a named tmux session split into 4 windows (tabs).
close_reason: Skeleton created: workspace.sh entry point, config/workspace.conf, scripts/tmux_helpers.sh

title: Create tmux notification script for inter-tab messaging
description: Write scripts/notify.sh that sends a visible message to the orchestrator pane (tab 1) via tmux send-keys. Used by the developer agent when escalating an overly broad plan phase. Message should include the beads issue ID and a short reason so the user knows what to act on.
close_reason: Created scripts/notify.sh — finds orchestrator pane by title, sends alert via tmux send-keys

title: Create git sync script (features <-> test branches)
description: Write scripts/sync.sh with two operations: (1) sync-to-test: rebase test/<name> onto features/<name> after developer closes a phase — triggered automatically by the developer agent; (2) sync-to-feature: rebase features/<name> onto test/<name> after debugger confirms all tests pass — triggered automatically by the debugger agent. Branch names passed as arguments.
close_reason: Created scripts/sync.sh with to-test and to-feature subcommands using git rebase

title: Write debugger agent CLAUDE.md and launch script
description: Create agents/debugger.md with role-specific instructions: read failing test-report beads issues, fix the code on test/<name> branch, signal tester to rerun, iterate until all tests pass, then trigger the test-to-feature branch sync and write a validation beads issue for the user. Create scripts/agents/debugger.sh to launch Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions.

title: Write tester agent CLAUDE.md and launch script
description: Create agents/tester.md with role-specific instructions: watch for new commits on features/<name> branch, write unit tests for each new feature, run them, write a test-report beads issue with pass/fail results. Create scripts/agents/tester.sh to launch Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions on the test/<name> branch.

title: Write developer agent CLAUDE.md and launch script
description: Create agents/developer.md with role-specific instructions: read beads plan-phase issues, implement one phase at a time on features/<name> branch, write only feature code (no tests), escalate via tmux message to orchestrator pane if the phase requires changes to more than 2 files. Create scripts/agents/developer.sh to launch Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions pointed at agents/developer.md.

title: Document full workflow in README.org
description: Update README.org with the complete multi-agent workflow: agent roles, branch conventions (features/<name>, test/<name>), beads issue types, the escalation path from developer to orchestrator, the sync triggers, and the final validation step by the user.
close_reason: README.org rewritten with full workflow, layout, file structure, and dispatch instructions

title: Wire agents into workspace.sh startup
description: Update workspace.sh to automatically launch the developer, tester, and debugger agent scripts in their respective panes on session start. Tab 1 (orchestrator) is left clean for the user. Each agent script should be launched as a background process within its pane so the pane remains interactive.
close_reason: Added agent launch commands to workspace.sh for developer, tester, debugger panes
